name: RF Transceiver Architecture
description: Sub-sector template for RF transceiver patents. Covers transmitter and receiver architectures (direct-conversion, superheterodyne), LO generation, mixer design, and integrated transceiver SoCs for wireless communication.
contextDescription: RF transceivers are in every wireless device — smartphones, WiFi routers, IoT sensors, and base stations. The transceiver converts baseband signals to/from RF. Broadcom, Qualcomm, Skyworks, and MediaTek are the dominant transceiver IC vendors. Direct-conversion (zero-IF) architecture dominates modern designs. Key innovations include wideband transceivers for 5G NR, calibration techniques (IQ imbalance, DC offset), and multi-band transceiver architectures. Detectable via chip teardown — transceiver die architecture is visible in SEM images.
scoringGuidance: Every wireless device contains at least one RF transceiver — billions of devices/year.
Direct-conversion architecture patents dominate modern designs — high infringement surface.
IQ calibration and DC offset cancellation are implemented in every transceiver.
Multi-band transceivers (single chip covering 4G+5G) are particularly valuable.
Transceiver SoC integration patents cover the trend toward fewer, more integrated chips.

questions.question: What transceiver architecture does this patent address? Score 8-10 for novel integrated transceiver architectures (wideband direct-conversion, multi-band single-chip), 5-7 for improvements to existing architectures (calibration, linearity), 3-4 for component-level innovation (mixer, LNA, PA) in transceiver context, 1-2 for no specific architecture.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Identify the specific transceiver architecture (direct-conversion, low-IF, superheterodyne) and innovations.

questions.question: Does this patent address transceiver calibration (IQ imbalance, DC offset, LO leakage, gain/phase mismatch)? Score 8-10 for novel calibration algorithms or adaptive compensation, 5-7 for standard calibration improvements, 3-4 for general signal quality, 1-2 for no calibration aspect.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Calibration is mandatory in every direct-conversion transceiver — high infringement frequency.

questions.question: Does this patent enable multi-band or wideband operation (covering LTE + 5G NR bands)? Score 8-10 for reconfigurable wideband transceiver covering sub-6 + mmWave, 5-7 for multi-band switching or tuning, 3-4 for dual-band, 1-2 for single-band only.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Modern smartphones need transceivers covering 30+ bands — multi-band is essential.

questions.question: What level of integration does this patent enable? Score 8-10 for full transceiver SoC (RF + baseband + PMU), 5-7 for multi-function RF front-end integration, 3-4 for component-level but with integration benefits, 1-2 for standalone component.
questions.reasoningPrompt: SoC integration is the trend — fewer chips, lower cost, smaller form factor.

questions.question: How detectable is this transceiver innovation via chip teardown? Score 8-10 for die-level architecture visible in SEM/optical (block diagram, calibration loops), 5-7 for circuit techniques detectable via probing, 3-4 for algorithm-level requiring firmware analysis, 1-2 for conceptual only.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Transceiver die layout is routinely analyzed in teardowns — high detectability.

questions.question: Does this patent address transceiver power efficiency (low-power modes, adaptive biasing, duty cycling)? Score 8-10 for novel power management techniques, 5-7 for power-aware design, 3-4 for general efficiency, 1-2 for no power focus.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Transceiver power consumption directly affects smartphone battery life.
